skill.name: launch-strategy
skill.description: Plan product launches, feature announcements, and release strategies using proven frameworks for building momentum and converting interest into users
skill.summary: Expert guidance for SaaS product launches using the ORB framework and five-phase launch approach to build momentum and convert interest into users
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Security audit confirms this skill is safe for publication. Static analyzer flagged 6 patterns as potential issues, but all are false positives. The skill consists entirely of markdown documentation describing launch strategy frameworks. No executable code, scripts, network requests, or file system operations are present. The flagged lines contain only prose text about marketing channels and launch phases.

content.value_statement: Many products fail to gain traction due to poor launch planning. This skill provides a structured five-phase approach and ORB framework to build momentum, capture attention, and convert interest into loyal users.
actual_capabilities: Guide users through the five-phase launch approach from internal testing to full launch
Apply the ORB framework to structure marketing across Owned, Rented, and Borrowed channels
Provide Product Hunt launch strategies with before, during, and after tactics
Help prioritize which updates deserve major announcements versus changelog mentions
Generate launch checklists tailored to pre-launch, launch day, and post-launch phases
Recommend questions to ask for understanding launch context and audience
limitations: Provides strategic guidance only; does not execute marketing campaigns or post to social platforms
Cannot access real-time analytics or performance data from your launch channels
Does not create visual assets, demo videos, or landing page designs
Recommendations are based on documented best practices, not industry-specific data

use_cases.title: SaaS Founder Planning First Launch
use_cases.description: A first-time founder uses this skill to structure their product launch from alpha testing through Product Hunt submission, building a waitlist and preparing launch assets across owned and rented channels.

use_cases.title: Product Manager Announcing Major Feature
use_cases.description: A PM at an established company applies the announcement prioritization matrix and post-launch tactics to maximize adoption of a new feature among existing users.

use_cases.title: Marketing Team Sustaining Launch Momentum
use_cases.description: A marketing team uses the ongoing launch strategy section to plan staggered announcements and maintain engagement after the initial launch spike.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Launch Planning
prompt_templates.prompt: I'm launching a new SaaS product in 6 weeks. Help me create a launch plan using the five-phase approach. My product is [describe product], my target audience is [describe audience], and I currently have [describe existing channels or audience size].

prompt_templates.title: Product Hunt Preparation
prompt_templates.prompt: I want to launch on Product Hunt next month. Walk me through the preparation checklist and help me optimize my listing. My product is [describe product] and my unique value proposition is [describe UVP].

prompt_templates.title: Channel Strategy Assessment
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze my current marketing channels using the ORB framework. Owned channels: [list]. Rented channels: [list]. Borrowed channel opportunities: [list]. Recommend where I should focus my efforts for an upcoming launch.

prompt_templates.title: Post-Launch Momentum Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: We launched last week and got initial traction. Help me create a post-launch plan to sustain momentum. Current metrics: [describe metrics]. Resources available: [describe team/budget]. Goals for next 30 days: [describe goals].

output_examples.input: User asks for a Product Hunt launch day checklist
output_examples.output: Product Hunt Launch Day Checklist: Treat it as an all-day event. Respond to every comment in real-time to boost visibility. Answer questions thoughtfully to spark discussions. Encourage your existing audience to engage with the listing. Direct traffic from Product Hunt back to your site to capture email signups. Monitor for technical issues and respond quickly to feedback.

output_examples.input: User asks how to decide what updates to announce
output_examples.output: Use the Announcement Priority Matrix: Major updates like new features or product overhauls deserve full campaigns across blog, email, in-app messages, and social media. Medium updates such as integrations or UI enhancements need targeted announcements to relevant segments via email and in-app banners. Minor updates like bug fixes belong in changelog and release notes to signal active development without dominating marketing.

best_practices: Build owned channels early and funnel all rented and borrowed channel traffic into them for long-term value
Treat launch as a phased process rather than a single event to build compounding momentum
Respond to every comment and engage actively on launch day rather than posting and disappearing
anti_patterns: Relying solely on rented channels like social media without building owned audience relationships
Launching everything at once instead of staggering announcements to maintain ongoing engagement
Treating Product Hunt as a one-day effort instead of preparing for weeks and engaging all day

faq.question: What is the ORB framework?
faq.answer: ORB stands for Owned, Rented, and Borrowed channels. Owned channels are assets you control like email lists and blogs. Rented channels are platforms like social media where algorithms control reach. Borrowed channels are other people's audiences through collaborations or guest content. The framework helps structure launch marketing across all three types.

faq.question: How long does a typical product launch take?
faq.answer: A full launch follows five phases: Internal Launch, Alpha Launch, Beta Launch, Early Access Launch, and Full Launch. This process typically spans 2 to 6 months depending on product complexity and audience size. Each phase builds momentum and validates the product with progressively larger user groups.

faq.question: Is Product Hunt worth it for my product?
faq.answer: Product Hunt works best for tech-savvy audiences and products with clear value propositions. Success requires weeks of preparation including building relationships, optimizing your listing, and engaging communities. If you can commit to all-day engagement and have a compelling product, it can provide credibility and early adopter exposure.

faq.question: What should I do after my launch day?
faq.answer: Post-launch work includes following up with engaged prospects, activating onboarding email sequences, publishing comparison pages against competitors, creating interactive demos, and planning your next launch moment. Include your announcement in roundup emails to catch people who missed the initial launch.

faq.question: How do I decide which updates to announce publicly?
faq.answer: Use the priority matrix: Major updates like new features get full multi-channel campaigns. Medium updates like integrations get targeted announcements to relevant segments. Minor updates like bug fixes go in changelogs. This prevents announcement fatigue while signaling active development.

faq.question: What owned channels should I prioritize?
faq.answer: Start with 1 to 2 based on your audience. If your industry lacks quality content, start a blog. If people want direct updates, focus on email. If engagement and community matter, build a Slack or Discord community. The key is choosing channels where your specific audience is active and converting all other traffic into these owned relationships.
